channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Montreal Victoire wins first PWHL Walter Cup with 4-0 victory over Ottawa Charge
item.description: The Montreal Victoire captured their first Professional Women's Hockey League (PWHL) Walter Cup championship on Wednesday, defeating the Ottawa Charge 4-0 in Game 4 of the best-of-five series. Abby Ro...

item.title: The Importance of Youth Participation in South Africa’s Democracy
item.description: In South Africa, there is growing concern about the disconnection of young people from politics. While older generations continue to queue at voting stations, remembering the sacrifices made for freed...

item.title: 19th-Century Photographs Documenting the Sámi People by Roche and Bonaparte
item.description: In 1884, Prince Roland Bonaparte organized an anthropological expedition to Northern Norway (Finnmark) and Sweden to document the Sámi people. Most of the photographs were taken by G. Roche, the exped...

item.title: India Raises Concerns About Pakistan's Conduct at UN Security Council
item.description: At the Annual UN Security Council Open Debate on the 'Protection of Civilians in Armed Conflict', India strongly criticised Pakistan, highlighting its history of violence against civilians. Harish Par...

item.title: Remembering Jane Birkin: Icon of 1960s–70s Fashion and Film
item.description: Jane Birkin (1946–2023) was an emblematic figure whose unique blend of British cool and French elegance defined the style of the 1960s and 1970s. Born in London and later embraced by Paris, she gained...

item.title: Pakistan Condemns India at UN Security Council Over Terrorism, Occupation and Minority Rights
item.description: During the recent United Nations Security Council debate on civilian protection, Pakistan strongly countered claims made by India regarding Pakistan’s conduct. India’s Permanent Representative alleged...

item.title: Trump Administration Establishes Fund for Allies Following IRS Lawsuit Settlement, Raising Legal and Ethical Questions
item.description: On May 20, 2026, the U.S. Department of Justice announced the creation of a $1.776 billion 'Anti-Weaponization Fund,' intended to compensate individuals who claim they were targeted for political pros...

item.title: Melbourne bars targeted with $10,000 monthly extortion amid ongoing attacks
item.description: Melbourne’s nightlife scene has been under a spate of violent attacks, with bar owners now facing extortion demands of $10,000 per month. Messages received by the businesses warn that payment will rem...

item.title: 1.7 Billion-Year-Old Fossils Reveal How Early Eukaryotes Evolved in Oxygenated Seas
item.description: Stored in an open-air warehouse in Darwin, Australia, are ancient mudstone cores containing fossils of microscopic organisms that lived over 1.7 billion years ago. These fossils, recently studied and ...

item.title: Senate Holds Proceedings as Impeachment Court for VP Sara Duterte
item.description: On May 21, 2026, the Philippine Senate convened to function as an impeachment court in the case against Vice President Sara Duterte. This historic session marks a critical moment in the country's poli...

item.title: IMF concludes Pakistan visit after reviewing economic reforms and FY2027 budget plans
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) mission has successfully wrapped up its week-long visit to Pakistan, during which officials held detailed discussions with Pakistani authorities about the country...

item.title: Smaller and Less Prestigious Roles Can Provide Valuable Career Experience
item.description: This editorial argues that overemphasizing brand names and elite institutions diminishes the value of genuine skills and responsibility. Students often pursue prestigious internships and roles, focusi...

item.title: Pakistan Condemns India at UN for Terrorism and Human Rights Abuses
item.description: At the United Nations Security Council (UNSC), Pakistan strongly criticised India for its role in exporting terrorism and persecuting minorities. Pakistan’s Counsellor, Saima Saleem, delivered a right...

item.title: Africa Forward Summit Seeks to Reframe France-Africa Relations
item.description: Di Africa Forward summit, wey France and Kenya co-host for Nairobi on May 11-12, 2026, na effort to renew di partnership between Africa and France, especially for innovation and growth. This year, dem...

item.title: Street Life in Vienna in the Mid-1980s Through Vintage Photography
item.description: The mid-1980s in Vienna were a unique period where the city appeared suspended between its grand imperial history and a modern world yet to fully embrace digital technology. Vintage photographs shared...

item.title: Ramaphosa Seeks Court Approval to Challenge Phala Phala Panel Findings
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has formally requested the Chief Justice's permission to launch a judicial review of the Section 89 Independent Panel report concerning the Phala Phala incident. Under Sectio...

item.title: Paul Schrader explores concept of a ‘Taxi Driver’ sequel involving AI companionship
item.description: Paul Schrader, the screenwriter behind the iconic 1976 film 'Taxi Driver', has discussed a potential idea for a sequel in which the protagonist, Travis Bickle, engages with an AI girlfriend. Schrader,...

item.title: Frank Zappa's Quick-Witted Comeback to Joe Pyne in 1966 Talk Show
item.description: In 1966, legendary musician Frank Zappa appeared on The Joe Pyne Show, a talk show famous for its confrontational style and aggressive host, Joe Pyne. Known for his provocative and sometimes insulting...

item.title: Sociological and Economic Factors Reducing the Likelihood of War Over Taiwan
item.description: The article examines the potential for a global conflict over Taiwan, emphasizing the importance of sociological and economic realities in shaping international relations. It analyzes Donald Trump's v...

item.title: Ebola Outbreak in Central Africa Worsens Amid US Aid Cuts
item.description: The ongoing Ebola outbreak in Central and East Africa is being exacerbated by significant reductions in US foreign aid and global health funding. Public health workers report severe shortages of essen...
